subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated clearly with comprehensive background and effective use of diagrams.
Methodology is described in step-by-step detail, facilitating replication.
Technical terms are employed accurately and explanations of stomatal function under stress are well framed.
weaknesses: Research question bundles multiple independent variables, diluting focus.
Justification for choice of specific treatment levels and statistical methods is brief and sometimes incomplete.
Control variables omit environmental humidity and light intensity measurements.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Based on strand scores (4 for communication, 3 for methodology, 3 for focus) the work sits solidly in the 3â4 band. Communication is accurate and effective, but the methodology lacks full rationale and the research question is overly broad. A mark of 4 reflects these strengths and limitations while respecting the 6-point scale.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., guard cells, osmotic stress).
Relevant application of a variety of sources, including primary literature and textbooks.
Accurate LaTeX presentation of chemical and statistical formulas demonstrates precise calculation.
weaknesses: Some key claims rely on conjecture rather than critically appraised evidence.
Referencing format is inconsistent and internet sources are not evaluated for reliability.
Application of source material occasionally remains descriptive rather than fully analytical.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate demonstrates accurate and consistent use of terminology (band 4) and mostly relevant application of sources (band 3). The integration of source material is clear but not always critically appraised, placing performance in the upper half of the 3â4 band. A mark of 4 reflects solid subject knowledge tempered by the noted gaps.

strengths: Quantitative data are handled with depthâmeans, standard deviations and non-parametric tests are applied.
Results are effectively visualized and linked to physiological mechanisms.
Limitations are acknowledged and improvements proposed, showing sound evaluative skills.
Argument is coherent with a logical narrative linking hypothesis, data, and conclusions.
weaknesses: Statistical reasoning contains errors (misinterpretation of p-values, lack of assumption checks).
Some evaluative comments remain descriptive, with overstatements of certainty.
Rationale for test selection and sampling sufficiency is not fully justified.
Argument is periodically diluted by the broad scope and occasional speculative assertions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand marks of 5 (analysis), 4 (evaluation), and 4 (argument) place the work solidly in the 4â6 band but below excellence. The depth of analysis and clear narrative justify a high band position; however, statistical misinterpretations and speculative leaps prevent top marks. On the 12-point scale, a score of 9 appropriately balances strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Structure follows EE conventions with clear headings, numbered figures, and integrated tables.
Layout is consistent with correct application of formatting conventions (fonts, captions, page numbers).
weaknesses: Occasional use of first-person narrative reduces formality.
Minor inconsistencies in heading labels and bilingual slips (Spanish/English) slightly detract.
Duplicate heading labels and uneven pagination appear in places.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both structure and layout reach the upper descriptors of the 1â2 band and touch on the 3â4 band standards. While the overall presentation is clear and navigable, formal tone and consistency issues prevent full marks. A score of 3 on the 4-point scale reflects strong but not flawless presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows a recognisable EE structureâtitle page with word count, abstract omitted (allowed under new guide), table of contents, introduction, methodology, results, discussion, conclusion and references. Headings and sub-headings guide the reader effectively, and data tables/figures are integrated in sequence. Minor issues (duplicate heading labels, uneven page numbering) do not impede comprehension. These qualities meet the highest band (3â4) for structure appropriateness, warranting the full 4.

Formatting conventionsâtitle page with complete information, consistent font, labelled figures, tables, and a contents pageâare largely observed. In-text citations and a bibliography are present, albeit with occasional inconsistencies. Graphs include titles, axes labels and error bars. Page numbers are provided. Minor typographic errors and some Spanish/English mixing do not significantly detract from readability. Overall, layout and formal presentation satisfy the upper descriptors of the 3â4 band, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

A logical narrative links background, hypothesis, data presentation and tentative conclusions, demonstrating coherence. Nevertheless, the argument is periodically diluted by the breadth of variables and by reliance on assertions not fully substantiated by data (e.g., speculations about mechanisms when statistics are non-significant). Some repetitive sections and occasional digressions reduce conciseness. Overall, the argument is clear and mostly coherent but exhibits minor inconsistencies, matching the descriptor for 4â6 band; a mark of 4 is assigned.

The candidate introduces the physiological importance of stomatal density and aperture, clearly explaining why these parameters matter for Spinacia oleracea under climate-related stresses. Background information, definitions and diagrams are supplied, allowing a non-specialist reader to grasp the topic. Occasional language slips (e.g. âpor lo que La densidadâ¦â) do not seriously hinder understanding, and technical terms (stomata, osmotic stress, transpiration) are employed with reasonable accuracy. Overall communication is clear and sufficiently thorough, fulfilling the descriptors for the 3â4 band and leaning toward the upper end, hence a mark of 4.

The essay cites a range of textbooks, web articles and a few primary research papers to build the theoretical framework and support discussion. Source material is generally relevant and integrated through paraphrase and occasional quotation. However, some key claims (e.g., explanations for unexpected trends) rely on conjecture rather than firmly referenced evidence, and the validity of internet sources is not critically appraised. Referencing is present but inconsistent in formatting. These patterns fit the 3â4 band, with clearer than minimal but not fully effective application; a score of 3 is given.

The student collects substantial quantitative data across 10 treatment groups and calculates means, standard deviations and non-parametric statistics. Graphs visualise trends effectively, and the analysis links patterns to biological mechanisms. Limitations (e.g., non-significant p-values) are acknowledged, though the statistical reasoning sometimes contains errors (misinterpretation of p-values and formula notation). Despite these weaknesses, the depth of data handling and attempt at inferential testing indicate a good, though not excellent, level of analytical skill. This aligns with the mid-upper area of the 4â6 band; a mark of 5 is appropriate.

The student describes a plausible experimental approach (multiple controlled seed trays, defined treatments, sample size of 20 imprints per treatment, basic statistical tests) and lists materials, variables and controls. Nevertheless, justification for choosing specific salinity levels, temperature steps and fertiliser dose is brief, and the designâs ability to isolate individual factors is weakened by limited replication at the seed-tray level. Statistical methods (Kruskal-Wallis, t-test) are mentioned but not always fully explained or correctly interpreted. These features correspond to an "appropriate though not fully comprehensive" methodology, meriting a solid 3 within the 3â4 band.

The discussion links findings to existing literature and evaluates possible physiological explanations. The student recognises limitations (short treatment duration, small treatment range, need for replication) and proposes improvements. However, some evaluative comments remain descriptive, and the significance of non-significant statistical outcomes is not fully explored. Conclusions occasionally overstate certainty given the p-values reported. The work shows clear, but not consistently well-supported, evaluationâcomfortably within the 4â6 band but closer to its lower end; hence a score of 4.

The research question is explicitly stated and linked to the stated aim of assessing how several environmental stresses influence stomatal traits. However, it bundles four independent variables (temperature, salinity, water stress, fertiliser) into one compound question, resulting in a scope that is wider than ideal for an EE. While each variable is quantified, the breadth reduces depth and makes complete focus on a single, sharply defined issue difficult. This aligns with the description of a clear but only partially focused RQ in the 3â4 band; a mid-band mark of 3 is appropriate.

Throughout the report the student employs subject-specific terminologyâstomatal density, guard cells, osmotic stress, Kruskal-Wallis testâaccurately and consistently. Conceptual explanations (e.g., turgor-mediated stomatal opening, osmotic potentials) demonstrate sound biological understanding. Minor language inaccuracies are largely linguistic rather than conceptual. This satisfies and slightly exceeds the expectations of the 3â4 band, so the upper mark of 4 is awarded.

annotations.comment: The calculation of NaCl mass using mEq conversions is accurate and uses clear LaTeX formatting for chemical quantification.
Formulas for mean and standard deviation are correctly presented in LaTeX, demonstrating precise mathematical computation.
The historical note on spinach origin adds little to the experimental focus. Streamline background content to maintain direct relevance to the research question.
Control variables table omits environmental humidity and light intensity measurements. Including these would enhance control and isolate factor effects.
The materials list is comprehensive, specifying equipment tolerances and concentrations, which supports reproducibility and procedural transparency.
Methodology is described in step-by-step detail, including seed tray setup and irrigation schedule, facilitating clear replication.
Photographs of weighing lack description of calibration or tare procedure. Documenting calibration ensures accuracy and reproducibility.
The inclusion of a word count on the title page meets formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to prescribed IB conventions.
The research question bundles multiple independent variables, diluting focus. Narrowing to one or two factors would allow deeper investigation and clearer conclusions.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically ordered, enabling easy navigation through the investigationâs major sections.
The introduction uses first-person narrative (âRealicÃ© este experimentoâ), which is less formal. Adopting an impersonal tone would improve academic presentation.
The introduction effectively communicates the significance of stomatal traits and frames the studyâs relevance under climate change conditions.
Terminology (e.g., guard cells, sub-stomatal chamber) is applied accurately, demonstrating clear understanding of stomatal anatomy and function.
The discussion on water stress integrates physiological mechanisms and is supported by literature, indicating sound biological understanding.
Projecting findings from a pea study ([12]) onto spinach without critical appraisal limits the strength of the inference. Consider species-specific evidence.
The source cited for stomatal density ([2]) is not appraised for currency or reliability. Critically evaluating source quality would strengthen your theoretical framework.
High-quality micrographs and clear, numbered captions enhance the visual presentation and aid reader comprehension of stomatal structures.
The rationale for choosing 20 nail polish imprints per sample to reduce uncertainty is stated but lacks quantitative justification of sampling sufficiency.
Listing five levels for salinity, five for temperature, two water regimes, and fertilizer creates an overly broad design. Reducing levels would increase focus and statistical power.
Statistical tests (t-test, Kruskal-Wallis) are mentioned without justification of test choice or assumption checks; explain why each test is appropriate.
